content:
type: SURVEY
files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Feature: validate_nesting param on Context. Context.__init__ (add validate_nesting param, _close_callbacks tracking of children), Context.close()/__exit__ (validation logic, RuntimeError if children open), _make_sub_context (inherit validate_nesting, register child with parent). shell_completion.py: _resolve_context and related context-creation call sites must pass validate_nesting=False.

content:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/core.py
note: I will own core.py: add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts, validate on close()/__exit__, RuntimeError with open children info. No other functional changes planned there beyond this feature.

fields.note: I will own core.py: add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts, validate on close()/__exit__, RuntimeError with open children info. No other functional changes planned there beyond this feature.

content:
type: DEFER
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: In _resolve_context (and any other Context(...) construction sites in this file, e.g. within shell_completion.py used for completion generation), explicitly pass validate_nesting=False when constructing Context objects (whether via Command.make_context(..., **extra) or Context(...) directly), e.g. ctx = cli.make_context(prog_name, args, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False) — same for the chained-group loop that resolves sub-contexts and any cmd.make_context(...) calls used for subcommand resolution. This ensures shell completion never triggers the new validate_nesting RuntimeError from core.py's Context.close()/__exit__, since completion doesn't always close contexts in strict nested order. If you wrap these calls in 'with' statements for your leak fix, still pass validate_nesting=False explicitly on each Context/make_context call so validation stays off regardless of cleanup order. Context.__init__ signature (my side, core.py) will be: def __init__(self, command, parent=None, ..., validate_nesting: bool = False) — validate_nesting is a plain keyword arg accepted by Context and forwarded by Command.make_context via **extra like other Context kwargs (e.g. resilient_parsing).
note: Owner: agent1. My feature only needs validate_nesting=False threaded into every Context-creation call in this file.

This ensures shell completion never triggers the new validate_nesting RuntimeError from core.py's Context.close()/__exit__, since completion doesn't always close contexts in strict nested order. If you wrap these calls in 'with' statements for your leak fix, still pass validate_nesting=False explicitly on each Context/make_context call so validation stays off regardless of cleanup order. Context.__init__ signature (my side, core.py) will be: def __init__(self, command, parent=None, ..., validate_nesting: bool = False) — validate_nesting is a plain keyword arg accepted by Context and forwarded by Command.make_context via **extra like other Context kwargs (e.g. resilient_parsing).

content:
type: SPEC
files: src/click/shell_completion.py
spec: Confirmed exact shape: Context.__init__ gains keyword-only-by-convention param 'validate_nesting: bool = False' (regular kwarg, not keyword-only enforced). Command.make_context(self, info_name, args, parent=None, **extra) forwards **extra straight into Context(...), so passing validate_nesting=False through make_context's **extra works exactly like resilient_parsing=False does today: cmd.make_context(name, args, parent=ctx, resilient_parsing=True, validate_nesting=False). Apply this kwarg at all 3 call sites you listed: (1) the top-level cli.make_context(...) call, (2) the non-chained cmd.make_context(...) call, (3) the chained-group cmd.make_context(...) call inside the loop. No other call sites in shell_completion.py construct Context/make_context directly (I greped for 'make_context(' and 'Context(' - only those 3). validate_nesting=False is also the class default, so even if you miss a spot it fails safe (no RuntimeError), but please add it explicitly at all 3 for clarity per the PR spec. No other asks.
note: Confirming for agent1's wrap-with-with implementation. I will not touch shell_completion.py.

No other call sites in shell_completion.py construct Context/make_context directly (I greped for 'make_context(' and 'Context(' - only those 3). validate_nesting=False is also the class default, so even if you miss a spot it fails safe (no RuntimeError), but please add it explicitly at all 3 for clarity per the PR spec. No other asks.

content:
type: DONE
files: src/click/core.py
note: core.py implementation complete: validate_nesting param on Context.__init__ (default False, inherited from parent), _make_sub_context registers children in parent._open_children when parent.validate_nesting is True, close() raises RuntimeError listing open children's info_name and deregisters self from parent on successful close. Verified with manual script: default behavior unaffected, validate_nesting=True raises/clears as expected. Ready for your shell_completion.py changes using validate_nesting=False kwarg as discussed.
